rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF ANITA SECKINGER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/10/2023 (no earlier date available) to be reviewed and adjudicated with the related trust accounting petition filed in PTR-17-300891. 1) No proof of payment of the assessment fee has been filed. 2) The petition must describe the services to be rendered on a periodic basis by the petitioner as conservator* and the reason why authority to make period payment is requested. A good faith estimate of the fees to be charged by the petitioner as conservator* until the next succeeding account required for the related trust must also be provided. Probate Code section 2643.1(b). *Note: If the petitioner intended to request authority to pay fees on account for his services as trustee of the related trust, the request must be made in the trust case as additional relief sought by the trust accounting petition. 3) No hourly fee schedule or statement of compensation has been filed re the petitioner's new rate ($200 per hour). Probate Code sections 2614.8 & 2643.1(a). 4) The telephone number for a Zoom appearance stated on the remote appearance information attached to the proof of service of notice must be updated.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDUARDA O. BUTAC
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/24/2023, pending a disposition of the related heirship petition. The following issues have not been addressed since the initial hearing on 12/12/2022: 1) No courtesy copy of the petition has been received. 2) No proposed order has been received. 3) No original statements of the bank accounts listed on Schedule F have been filed. See LRSF 14.34(D). 4) A copy of the petition must be served on the person who requested special notice. Item 5 of the proof of service attached to the DE-120 form must be checked. See Probate Code section 1252(a). 5) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form. 6) Please file a copy of the closing escrow statement for the sale of the estate real property.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JUNE PUTNAM GOLDSMITH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/03/2023. 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) The status report (petition) must be verified by the administrator. 3) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form. 4) The box at item 1 of the DE-120 form must be checked. 5) Direct notice must be given to each minor beneficiary, as this is not a protective proceeding. See CRC 7.51(d). 6) If the decedent's Chase bank accounts listed on the inventory and appraisal turned out to be joint accounts not belonging to this estate, a corrected inventory and appraisal must be filed, with a copy served on the person who requested special notice. See Probate Code section 1252(b). 7) No DE-174 form has been filed in response to the creditor claim. 8) All the information required by LRSF 14.35(J)(1) must be provided. 9) The petition must be amended and renoticed to pray for a continuance of administration for a specific period. See LRSF 14.35(J)(2).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RUTH LUCAS PARDINI
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (533 Gates St)
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following procedural issues must be cleared for the court to proceed. 1. Publication is not in compliance with PC sec.10300. (1) Private sale must occur no earlier than the day after publication is completed. Publication set forth that bids or offers will be received at any time after the first publication (5/2/23); however, it appears this bid was received before the date as petition was executed on 4/21/23. (2) "Notice of intention to sell real property" should have been published pursuant to GC sec. 6063a. LRSF 14.31. 2. Notice was not given to the purchasers. [Probate Code Section 10308(c).] The court may allow the buyer to waive notice of hearing if she appears. 3. Date of valuation of reappraisal. I&A for reappraisal filed on 5/26/23, showing the property was valued as of the date of death. 4. Name of the company/office, if any, of the buyer's agent 5. Petition has Attachment 4f stating terms of sale which were not included in the publication. 6. Allegation if Petitioner has given Notice of Proposed Action of entering into the exclusive listing agreement to the other heir, Diana Lucas. Notice of Hearing on this petition to Diana Lucas is not required; however, Petitioner should have given notice on her action related to this sale to Diana Lucas. 7. Sale date to be specified on No.4.c. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROGER J. GUETTINGER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: REPOSTED 06/02/2023. Continued to 7/10/2023 at the judicial officer's instruction after review. A verified supplement must be filed addressing the following information: (1) Date and place of domestic partnership (2) Specific information regarding the manner and timing (a date) of acquisition of each property alleged to be community property (3) Allegation regarding ownership of any real and personal property on date of domestic partnership and a description and approximation of value (4) Decedent's net worth at time of domestic partnership (5) A copy of any document establishing the character of the property (6) Notice of remote appearance to the decedent's heirs. LRSF 14.1.D.(6). The document needs to be filed two weeks prior.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BRUCE EDWARD BENHAM
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/10/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The wrong form of Notice of Hearing (DE-115 instead of DE-120) was served. (2) A Second Supplement to the petition must be filed, signed and verified by the petitioner, completing item 5a(2) of the petition. (3) The copy of the Letters attached to the petition were not certified within 60 days of the filing of the petition. (4) Does the personal representative of Ursula Elisabeth Benham's estate not need to have this decedent's 50% community property interest confirmed as passing to the surviving spouse as well as the surviving spouse's 50% community property interest confirmed as belonging to her? Courtesy copies of all papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 6/28/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MAKO UCHIDA ROVA REVOCABLE TRUST DATED 5/24/05
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Remove Trustee, To Appoint Successor Trustee, And To Compel Trustee To Account
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/3/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been submitted or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (3) Whenever an instrument written in a foreign language is offered for probate or attached as an exhibit to a pleading, it must always be accompanied by a copy translated into English. All translations must be accompanied by a declaration setting forth the translator's qualifications and/or credentials. [LRSF 14.15 D.] (All petitions involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries. [CRC 7.902.] It appears, however, that the petitioner is now the sole beneficiary; however, Counsel should take note of this rule.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 6/22/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARGARET ANN BURY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/10/2023. No response to notes sent 04/26/2023.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES M. DEIGNAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/10/2023. The following procedural issues must be cleared two weeks prior: (1) Trustee to be identified and trust beneficiary to be identified if the proposed executor is the sole trustee. If so, Notice of Petition to Administer [DE-121] needs to be sent to all trust beneficiary accordingly. (2) Duties and Liabilities to list the proposed personal rep's telephone number (3) Clarification if the decedent's will is self-proving will or not. Petition #3.f.(2) is silent. (4) Petition #8 to identify the trustee of the Deignan Family Trust. If the proposed personal rep is the sole trustee, trust beneficiaries must be identified and noticed. (5) Notice of Petition to Administer [DE-121] needs to be given to all named executors. PC 8100(b).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YVONNE S. HALEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIAM T. HAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing to admit the will. No appearance required. Status hearing on filing inventory and appraisal is set for 10/02/2023. Status on close of estate is set for July 15, 2024.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARIANA GANTUS WALL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/10/23. Petition to supplement the following procedural issues: (1) Appears the publication set forth the hearing date as 6/6/2023, not 6/5/2023. Proof of re-publication is needed. (2) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License to be filed. LRSF 14.18.E. (3) Date of birth of each minor heir to be listed on Petition #8. (4) Need allegation whether the court should appoint GALs for the two minor children whose names not listed on the decedent will. PC 21621. Supplemental documents must be filed two weeks prior.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WUN YEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BRIAN DEAN BENNETT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, subject to requirement of minimum bond ($20,000) with consent of counsel (via e-mail on 06/01/2023). Status hearing on close of estate will be set for 07/08/2024.
